commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Experiment 9: chained verification tasks break coherent error immunity

Key result: embedding a verification step within coherent-error tasks
restores truth bias to 71% (vs 43% for isolated coherent, p < 1e-6).
This is the strongest evidence that cross-domain dependencies are the
specific mechanism through which compression prefers truth.

- 4 models (tiny, seeds 42-45), 6 chain types
- Per-type: arithmetic 96%, factoring 90%, solve 88%, quadratic 61%,
  derivative 53%, tangent 35%
- Control: same models on standard coherent test = 43% (no bias)
- Figure 10, Table 10/10a in both paper versions
- Updated model count: 150+
- Chained tasks moved from Future Work to completed experiment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: compression-drives-truth
repository.description: Compression Truth Bias: индуктивное смещение языковых моделей к истине через давление сжатия

commit.message:
Add wallet-to-PID binding, onboarding wizard, and comprehensive tests

- crypto/binding.py: create/verify dual-signature (SIWE + Ed25519) bindings
- onboarding/wizard.py: first-run state machine with Gopher menu flow
- onboarding/server.py: minimal TCP server for wizard before normal startup
- db/schema.py: pid_bindings table (append-only with immutability triggers)
- tests/test_binding.py: 7 tests covering creation, verification, tampering, immutability
- tests/test_onboarding_wizard.py: 7 tests covering is_onboarding_complete, wizard steps, state persistence
- pillar.py: onboarding gate on startup, WebSocket bridge + IPC wired into task group
- integration/websocket_bridge.py: onboarding_connect/signature handlers for browser extension
- core/gopher_server.py: /identity.json endpoint, wizard routing when onboarding incomplete
- auth/session.py: chain_id support for SIWE challenges
- mesh/discovery.py: onion address in peer announcements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add policy engine and baseline solver foundation (Phase 3 & 4)

Phase 3: Rule-based policy engine with 6 typed evaluators (SiblingCohesion,
MinBlockLength, SchoolNightRoutine, TravelDistanceLimit, ExchangeLocation,
ActivityCommitment), policy registry, applicability filtering, evaluation
engine with HARD/STRONG/SOFT priority tiers, parameter validation, and
scope-based filtering (FAMILY/CHILD/DATE_RANGE/CHILD_DATE_RANGE).

Phase 4: Deterministic baseline schedule solver with normalized input
builder, constraint model, greedy solver with 5 weight profiles for
candidate diversity, tiered weighted objective system (stability, family
structure, fairness + secondary placeholders), policy integration,
candidate extraction with exchange inference, deterministic ranking
with tie-breaking, deduplication, and structured artifact generation.

127 tests passing across both phases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Read the Docs build warnings

- Remove duplicate changelog entry from second toctree in index.rst
- Remove html_static_path for non-existent _static directory from conf.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Moved org.gradle.java.home to user-level ~/.gradle/gradle.properties
so GitHub Actions (Ubuntu) can use its own JDK setup.

commit.message:
Redirect Netlify Identity tokens to /admin/ in Next.js

In Next.js the identity widget only lives in /admin/index.html, but
Netlify sends invite/recovery links to the site root. This script
detects the token in the URL hash and redirects to /admin/ where
the widget can handle it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: round 2 — error handling, race conditions, dead code, React keys (#106)

- Wrap 7 API routes in try-catch (admin/errors, bookmarks, chat, integrations, subscription, tos)
- Fix workspace creation race condition with transaction + re-check
- Fix bookmark collection limit TOCTOU with atomic transaction
- Fix Polar webhook idempotency fallback (crypto hash vs Date.now)
- Replace unstable index-based React keys with stable identifiers
- Remove dead code (HoverLift, AnimatedNumber components)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pipeline: drop web search, switch to Haiku, normalize coverage values

Root cause: web_search_20250305 responses consume 20k+ tokens each, instantly
exhausting the account's 30k TPM limit with 10 parallel nodes — yielding
0 companies. Additionally, all claude-sonnet-4-5 quota was exhausted.

Fix:
- Remove Step 2A web search entirely; Stage 2 is a single memory-based call
  per node (same quality prompts, no rate limit pressure)
- Switch both stages to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (higher TPM ceiling,
  sufficient for structured JSON tasks)
- Add coverage value normalizer (Haiku returns "high" instead of "heavy")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architect + developer knowledge for Sprint 3

architect/knowledge.md:
- Updated tech stack (React 19, Tailwind, TanStack Query, bcrypt 4.x direct)
- Added input_bindings JSONB column to nodes schema
- Replaced Mode 2 (folder-read+DB) with actual file-based pipeline (Mode 2)
- Added planned DB-load pipeline as Mode 3
- Updated script contract to reflect ./input/ + ./output/ + env vars pattern
- Added implemented API endpoints section (deviations from v0.2 design)
- Added ADR-013 through ADR-017 (file pipeline, input_bindings, seeded scripts, config files, polling)

developer/knowledge.md:
- Full rewrite with actual confirmed stack and patterns
- State machine transitions and auto-trigger chain documentation
- Input bindings schema with filename-pin pattern
- Script contract with config/ dir pattern
- Executor logic (seeded vs uploaded detection)
- Technical decisions log with Sprint 3 decisions
- Common pitfalls table from lessons learned

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Default /balance date to tomorrow (next day)

date, so using tomorrow means "end-of-today balance". A custom date
can still be set by prefixing the message with a YYYY-MM-DD line.

commit.message:
Initial commit: LLC Shottie artist website

Single-page site for LLC Shottie (Albuquerque, NM rapper).
Features his real album artwork from Deezer CDN, all 11 tracks,
streaming links, social media, and booking contact form.
LoLyfe Entertainment / Burque Records.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(nearest-derive): add Flat bounds to unbounded type params in emit impl (#32)

* fix(nearest-derive): add Flat bounds to unbounded type params in emit impl

The generated `make()` methods were using `split_for_impl()` which only
includes bounds explicitly written by the user. Type parameters without
an explicit `Flat` bound (e.g. from a where clause) were missing the
required `Flat` constraint on the impl block, causing compilation errors
in downstream usage.

* style: format imports in emit derive

Optimize planet rendering: two-tier textures, vectorized noise, background preload

Main view uses low-res 32×64 textures (16x fewer pixels), while high-res
128×256 textures are generated on a background worker thread for all visible
points. Vectorized Perlin noise fallback (_perlin3_batch) eliminates the
per-pixel Python loop when the C noise library is unavailable (~58x faster).

The BUILD phase didn't explicitly read tickets.branching from
task-status.json before entering the loop. After context compaction
between BRANCH_SETUP and BUILD, the orchestrator lost the "per-task"
choice and defaulted to single-branch behavior. Added MANDATORY STATE
LOAD blocks to tdd.md, lemongrab.md, and resume.md.

commit.message:
feat: add 0x69 protocol discriminator byte to HOW binary frames

Prefix every HOW binary frame with discriminator byte 0x69 so HOW
messages can coexist with other binary protocols on the same WebSocket
connection. Marshal prepends the byte, unmarshal validates and strips it.

fix: backend test accesses lazy attrs before restoring QGIS mocks (0.1.0b65)

_compat uses PEP 562 __getattr__ — backend detection runs on first attr
access, not during reload().  The test was accessing RASTERIO_AVAILABLE
after the finally block restored qgis mocks to sys.modules, so
in_osgeo_environment() saw the mock and chose GDAL.

Move attr access inside the try block while qgis is still removed.

commit.message:
fix: wrap all NDK clang variants for ccache on Android CI (#384)

The previous HEREDOC-based wrapper had two issues:
1. YAML indentation added leading spaces to the shebang line,
   making the wrapper scripts non-executable
2. Only bare clang/clang++ were wrapped, but CMake uses
   target-triple prefixed variants (e.g. aarch64-linux-android24-clang)

Switch to printf for correct shebang generation and glob all clang
variants in the NDK bin directory.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: massively expand allowlist — remove ~476 false positives

Deep audit found legitimate domains being blocked across categories:
legal streaming (Pluto, Tubi, Plex, Bilibili, NicoNico, etc.),
music platforms (Qobuz, Discogs, Last.fm, radio.garden), manga/comic
publishers (VIZ, Kodansha, Webtoons, Marvel, Dark Horse), government
sites (.gov, .edu), open-source tools (FFmpeg, mpv, Jellyfin, Kodi),
news outlets (BBC), reference sites (Rotten Tomatoes, TVTropes),
developer platforms (Hugging Face, XDA), and many more.

Blocklist: 2259 → 1783 domains.

Fix Lidarr submission flooding and improve queue resilience

Disable auto-search when adding artists to Lidarr to prevent flooding
the download queue with every album by every artist. Artists are now
added as shell entries (monitor: none, searchForMissingAlbums: false),
while individual album submissions trigger searches under backpressure
control.

- Validate artist name before submission (fixes "Artist must not be empty")
- Detect permanent failures (empty names, path conflicts, MBID not found)
  and skip retries immediately
- Queue cleanup now clears stale lidarr_status on entities so UI badges
  don't show "queued" forever after permanent failures
- Bump defaults: queue_max 20→50, submit_interval 30s→3m
- Rewrite Lidarr enricher docs with full pipeline architecture

commit.message:
RE: document PhyAccess classes — DirectPhyAccess and FWPhyAccess

Both share identical vtable layout: phyPower/lowPower/advertise/sleep.
DirectPhyAccess uses bRequest=0x31 (PHY power), bRequest=0x32 (Clause 45
MDIO read/write), and a 9-step PHY register sequence for speed advertisement
(MMD7 + Aquantia vendor regs 0xc430-0xc432). FWPhyAccess uses a single
bRequest=0x61 with a 4-byte control struct where individual bits encode
power/sleep/advertise state. Documents vendorCmd() helper and all new
vendor command IDs.

commit.message:
Refactor payment flow to use Stripe PaymentElement and clean up checkout/login/cart logic

Replaces custom card element handling with Stripe's unified PaymentElement, simplifies payment page styles, and updates related checkout, login, cart, and admin API code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Canonicalize paths in SBPL profile generation

SBPL checks against real (resolved) paths, so symlinks like
/tmp -> /private/tmp must be resolved before adding to the profile.
Without this, sandbox rules for symlinked paths silently fail to
match, causing sandboxed processes to be denied legitimate access.

deploy.yml:
- BACKEND_DEPLOYMENT_LABEL default: app=backend → app=options-backend
- Validate secrets: add AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING + KEY_VAULT_URL checks
- aks-set-context: replace invalid commit SHA with @v4 tag
- Image substitution: use grep -rl to find only manifests with __IMAGE_TAG__
  (replaces brittle find over non-existent infra/k8s/backend/ directory)
- New step: create/update options-backend-secrets K8s secret imperatively
  from GitHub secrets before applying manifests
- kubectl apply: fix path infra/k8s/backend/ → infra/k8s/ (×2)
- Frontend cache-dependency-path: frontend/package-lock.json →
  frontend/vue-app/package-lock.json
- Frontend working-directory: ./frontend → ./frontend/vue-app (×2)
- SWA app_location: / → frontend/vue-app
- SWA output_location: frontend/dist → dist
